text.title: IaC Testing Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `IaC Testing Patterns`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every PR MUST run fmt -check + validate + linter (tflint / pulumi preview --diff); any failure blocks merge.
rule.rationale: Fast feedback at the cheapest layer of the test pyramid.

rule.statement: Org policies (no public buckets, CMEK required, no primitive roles) MUST be enforced via policy-as-code (OPA / Sentinel / Checkov) in CI; manual review alone is forbidden.

rationale.source: Open Policy Agent docs, HashiCorp Sentinel docs

rule.rationale: Manual policy enforcement misses subtle violations and decays under load.

rule.statement: Production-bound modules MUST have at least one integration test that applies + destroys against a real cloud sandbox.
rule.rationale: Provider-side validation differs from local; integration tests catch what plan cannot.

rule.statement: Integration tests MUST destroy resources at teardown even on failure; orphaned resources are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Cost leaks accumulate quickly + auditor flags abandoned cloud resources.

rule.statement: Component-level tests MUST assert specific plan properties (resource counts, key attribute values), not just exit code 0.
rule.rationale: Exit-0 plans still produce wrong resources; attribute assertions catch shape drift.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
